text:
Market today: Jobs badly missed (57K vs expectations), which cuts both ways - slower economy hurts earnings, but rate-cut hopes get a boost. Tech/chips caught in the middle; the macro mood is cautious but not panicked. Margin traders should stay alert.

Your holdings: CRWD is the emergency - you're down 72.83% and the stop is $590 on a $195 stock, which looks like a legacy stop that never got updated. That stop is useless right now. SOXL is down 28.83% and already below its $200 stop - it's triggered, leveraged decay works against you daily. SPAL is a new leveraged ETF already down 15.49% and sitting right at its $21.38 stop - margin amplifies every tick here.

Watch: Whether the weak jobs number drives a genuine rate-cut rally in tech, or whether recession fear wins. Also watch SOXL's behavior at open - it's already stopped out on paper.

Bottom line: Cut the bleeding leveraged positions first, then let the jobs data tell you whether tech gets a bid or rolls over.
plays: SOXL: Already below $200 stop - exit or have a plan at open. Leveraged decay plus a margin account is a compounding problem; don't let hope override the stop.
CRWD: Your $590 stop is stale - price is $195. Decide your real exit level now or this position has no risk management at all.
SPAL: At $21.38 stop right now. On margin, a leveraged ETF this close to its stop with a 15% loss already - be ready to cut immediately if it breaches $21.38 at open.
